title: Olive & Herb Buttery Rice
summary: Fresh, briny, and loaded with goodness — this buttery herb recie is a great base for so many meals.

method.description: Finely slice the garlic and chop the herbs. Wash the rice two or three times until the water runs clear.
Heat a medium-sized saucepan over medium heat and add the butter. Once melted, add the garlic and cook for 8 mins until caramelised.
Stir in the olives, a pinch of salt, and a pinch of sugar (if using). Cook for another minute.
Tip in the washed rice, then pour in the chicken stock (or water). Bring to a boil, then cover with a lid and reduce the heat to as low as possible.
Cook for 12 mins, then turn off the heat and let the rice sit for 15 mins.
Once ready, fluff the rice with a fork, mix through the chopped herbs, and season to taste with salt and black pepper. Serve immediately

tips: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days.
To freeze, portion into containers and freeze for up to 3 months. Defrost in the fridge overnight and reheat in a pan or microwave.
Great for meal prep—use as a base for bowls, salads, or protein-rich meals throughout the week.
Swap jasmine rice for basmati or long-grain rice.
Use vegetable stock instead of chicken stock for a vegetarian option.
Try black or kalamata olives for a deeper, richer flavour.

chefs.name: Finn Tonry
chefs.summary: Finn Tonry (@Finntonry) is a chef and recipe developer who’s all about making healthy food exciting. After years working in some of London’s best restaurants, he’s now focused on creating high-protein dishes that look great, taste amazing, and actually make you feel good.
